docs.text:
• risk perception ? anxiety
  • automated cars in general ? shuttle specifically
  • unified models
  • ways to link anxiety/.. with established models
  • industry report ? academic conference paper

docs.title: Month 1 activities
docs.text: Activity Description Training 3-day mandatory training; research integrity, etc. 24 hours in total Lab Visit ALIVE, VR Cave, Design Factory MKCC Project help create forms; review 2018 report PGR Forms Learning Agreement Form; Development Needs Analysis Form Refine objectives and 1st year plan Research Understand key concepts Systematic literature review 1-1 Meeting Had 5 one-on-one meetings with Jisun

docs.title: Key concepts
docs.text:
1. Autonomous Vehicles in general

  2. technology

    how they work, issues, industry

  3. acceptence

    psychological issues > technological issues

    it is the psychological issues rather than the technological ones that led to the obstacles to the large-scale adoption (Sjöberg, 2002; Xu et al., 2018) Tan et al, 2022 - population heterogeneity- gender, age, mobile behavioural characteristics, income - behavioural theories / models - trust, perceived risk/benefit, attitude, perceived ease of use/usefulness, social norm, compatibility - dynamic - willingness to use - technology anxiety - self-identity expressed by driving - affective reactions Tan et al, 2022 - anxiety, pleasure, etc. - methods - some surveys restricted to people with driving licence only, e.g. Wang et al, 2020 - models

  4. Passenger Anxiety

    influencing factors

    survey design

    mostly driver-oriented

  5. HMIs

    principles

    mostly static rather than interactive, e.g. Bolzmacher et al., 2025

    self-report

  6. Simulation

    AR, VR, AR within VR

    cognitive workload
